edge: The context discusses the challenge of generalized medical image segmentation
The domain shift problem refers to the significant distribution discrepancy between images from various sources.
Existing methods focus on learning shape-invariant representations to address the domain shift issue.
An ideal generalized representation exhibits similar pattern responses within the same channel across domains.
The network aims to handle the significant distribution discrepancy by capturing similar patterns in multiple channels.
The proposed method leverages channel-wise decoupled deep features to address this issue.
The cross-attention mechanism helps mine the long-range dependency between deep and shallow features.
A relaxed deep whitening transformation is introduced to facilitate learning channel-wise decoupled features.
The decoupled feature query (DFQ) scheme is seamlessly integrated into the Transformer segmentation model for end-to-end learning.
The DFQ scheme demonstrates superior performance
DFQ is proposed to be integrated into Transformer models for better domain generalization performance.
Medical image segmentation has seen rapid development due to the strength of deep learning techniques.

RDWT is introduced to improve the representation capacity and reduce channel redundancy.
